3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.82 3.63 4.06 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.68 0.64 0.73 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.75 0.81 0.79 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.39 2.19 2.54 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.43 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.06 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

3.54
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.49
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.76
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.29
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio

facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-01-16 12 12 0 48 1 48
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-09-21 4 4 0 48 1 48
Total weighted health score
48.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (26)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

24 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-01-16 2025-03-10
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2025-01-16 2025-03-10
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2025-01-16 2025-03-10
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-01-16 2025-03-10
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-01-16 2025-03-10
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-01-16 2025-03-10
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-01-16 2025-03-10
0745 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. D 2025-01-16 2025-03-10
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-01-16 2025-03-10
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2025-01-16 2025-03-10
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2025-01-16 2025-03-10
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-01-16 2025-03-10
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2023-09-21 2023-10-21
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. G 2023-09-21 2023-10-21
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. G 2023-09-21 2023-10-21
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2023-09-21 2023-10-21
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2022-05-26 2022-06-24
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2022-05-26 2022-06-24
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2022-05-26 2022-06-24
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2022-05-26 2022-06-24
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2022-05-26 2022-06-24
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2022-05-26 2022-06-24
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2022-05-26 2022-06-24
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2022-05-26 2022-06-24
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2022-05-26 2022-06-24
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. D 2022-05-26 2022-06-24
